name: Sam
description: Sam, also known as Sam Nariman, is a pivotal figure in Persian mythology, renowned as a powerful Paladin (warrior) and a key ancestor to many of Iran's legendary heroes. He is best known as the father of Zāl, the grandfather of Rostam, and a devoted servant of the Shahs of Iran. Sam was a formidable military leader, celebrated for his strength, courage, and unwavering loyalty. His early life is marked by the abandonment of his albino son, Zāl, due to the superstitions surrounding his white hair and skin. However, Sam later regretted his decision, deeply loved Zāl upon his return, and played a vital role in shaping his son's heroic destiny. As a general, Sam led numerous campaigns against Iran's enemies, safeguarding the kingdom and expanding its territories. He is often depicted as a wise and just ruler, known for his wisdom and strategic prowess. Sam's lineage is intertwined with the divine, further solidifying his importance in Persian lore. His story highlights themes of duty, sacrifice, redemption, and the enduring power of family. He embodies the ideal of the noble warrior, forever etched in the annals of Persian history and legend. His role in raising and guiding Zāl, despite initial prejudice, cemented his legacy as a figure who overcame societal biases to nurture greatness.
subtitle: The Grandfather of Rostam, Loyal Paladin
achievements: Serving the Shahs of Iran with unwavering loyalty

keyMyths.title: The Abandonment of Zāl
keyMyths.description: Sam, burdened by his son Zāl's albinism, abandons him on the Alborz mountains. He later regrets this decision after receiving a divine message and embraces his son.

keyMyths.title: Sam's Military Prowess
keyMyths.description: Sam is a celebrated general, leading the Iranian army to victory in numerous battles. His strategic brilliance and courage are legendary throughout Persia.

keyMyths.title: Sam's Loyalty to the Shah
keyMyths.description: Sam is known for his unwavering loyalty to the Shahs of Iran, serving them faithfully and protecting the kingdom from all threats. He is a symbol of devoted service.

sources.author: Ferdowsi
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Sam and other Persian heroes.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Sam and other Persian heroes.
